reasoning: The forecast horizon is extremely short, with only 66 days remaining between the knowledge cutoff (October 26, 2025) and the resolution date (end of 2025). Evidence overwhelmingly suggests Musk will remain CEO in the near term: in May 2025, Tesla Board Chair Robyn Denholm firmly denied reports of a CEO search, and Musk publicly committed to leading the company for at least five more years. Furthermore, Musk exited his government role in late May 2025 to dedicate more time to Tesla. The reported $1 trillion pay package in September 2025 strongly implies long-term alignment, and while it requires a formal succession plan, such plans are rarely executed with less than a few months' notice unless there is an emergency. Barring an unforeseen health crisis, sudden scandal, or abrupt title change, Musk is highly unlikely to step down by the end of 2025.
